proposals.spec_text: Include shape definitions in state streams only on first use
proposals.actual: All registered shapes included in every state stream update
proposals.proposed_text: FR-006b: The server MUST cache registered shapes and include all registered shape definitions in every state stream update, so all clients (including late-joiners) receive the complete shape cache without per-subscriber tracking.
proposals.rationale: Deliberate simplification. Per-subscriber cache tracking adds complexity with minimal benefit at current scale. Implementation is functionally correct.

proposals.spec_text: Render each shape type with geometry that visually matches the physics collider dimensions
proposals.actual: Triangle, Compound, and Mesh shapes render as placeholder spheres
proposals.proposed_change: Implement custom MeshDraw generation in ShapeGeometry.fs for Triangle (3-vertex face), Compound (child entities with local transforms), and Mesh (triangle list vertex data)
proposals.rationale: Placeholder spheres are misleading for debug/development. The spec requirement for visual accuracy is fundamental to the viewer's purpose.

proposals.spec_text: Users MUST be able to set the position and velocity of kinematic bodies directly
proposals.actual: Position/velocity only settable at creation time; no runtime update RPC
proposals.proposed_change: Add SetBodyPose proto message and command (body_id, position, orientation, velocity, angular_velocity) with SimulationWorld.setBodyPose handler and client interface wrappers
proposals.rationale: Kinematic bodies are designed for script-driven motion. Without runtime pose updates, they are limited to constant-velocity motion from creation, severely limiting utility.

proposals.spec_text: All new features accessible through REPL commands, MCP tools, scripting library
proposals.actual: Core features accessible; scripting library has 4/10 constraint convenience builders
proposals.proposed_text: SC-008: All new features MUST be accessible through the REPL and MCP interfaces. The scripting library MUST provide convenience builders for the most commonly used constraint types (ball-socket, hinge, weld, distance limit) with the remaining types constructible via standard proto message builders.
proposals.follow_up: Add 6 remaining constraint convenience builders as enhancement
proposals.rationale: Core accessibility met via REPL and MCP. Scripting convenience builders cover the 4 most common types. Remaining types accessible via proto API.
